A nice alternative to the auto on off of the pro, if you happen to have a monitor/TV supporting it, is to use on of the usb ports on the display to power the light. Many displays only send power to these ports when the display is on, so the light will turn on and off with the display you're using. You'll need a 1.5 Amp port, which often gets labelled as HDD (for connecting a harddrive) on TVs.

I personally use an alternative to both of these, as I wanted a wireless controller, but wasn't a fan of the one for the halo. Will you be considering doing a video on bias lighting or computer glasses? I've been using Power Practical's white bias lighting, and Gunnars Intercept for the last several years and I can comfortable tell you that I can play games 1-2ft away from my 32in monitor for many, many hours on end without any eye strain.

If you've never heard of bias lighting, it's kind of an optical illusion where having rear-lit display will reduce strain on your eyes while also improving the perceived contrast of your display. It's a win-win. There was a short period where my bias light died, and it was incredibly uncomfortable using my monitor for more than 30min lol.

I'm going to preface this by saying that overall you've produced a very good video. Your presentation is concise, well organized, and well shot. I do, however, want to comment that I find that the continual flashing of your hands and fingers is quite distracting and irritating.

If you have a glossy monitor DO NOT get the xiaomi. The BenQ is phenomenal because the light doesn't cause additional glare from a glossy monitor. This is the #1 reason to get the lightbar in the dark especially.

The only thing that really impressed me about the xiaomi over is the non touch controls as the tactile feeling makes a huge difference when using the button in the dark. 

BenQ's backlight is essential if your monitor doesn't have a backlight as the backlight is needed to add depth and reduce eye strain, Xiaomi is front light only. 

I'd say wait for the BenQ on sale, or add some backlight yourself using LED strips and pair this with the Xiaomi. Best bang for your buck.

The Quntis doesn't really glare on the screen, but it illuminates every tiny speck of dust and still shines in my eyes. 
The dust was driving me mad. I cleaned it every day. 

I just got the Benq and the difference is night and day!

The light is so focused that it doesn't light up the dust and doesn’t shine in my eyes. 
The backlight is a nice addition.

こないだ買って使ってます。個人的デメリットを勝手に補足します。

・自動調光
当然、本製品の真上の光を基準に調光される。よって、天井照明によってできた自分自身の影は反映されない。つまり、自動調光では手元が暗いと感じた。
公式HPに"自動的に500ルクスに照らします"とあるが、それは影がない真下の話。実際にアプリで計測したが、席を外すと500ルクス前後、席に座ると自分の影ができて暗くなる。
天井照明との位置関係で、机に自分自身の影がある環境の方は無意味な機能となるため注意が必要。

・不自由
本製品で光沢紙の印刷物等を見ると光が反射して見づらい。アームのあるデスクライトであれば光を印刷物の真上に持っていくことで対処できるが、これはできない。

本動画で挙げられていたデメリットと上記が問題ないようでしたら、スクリーンバー式のデスクライトとして優秀だと思います。

I remember seeing these a few years ago. I am with you on the protecting the eyes thing. I had dry eyes so bad a few years back that I had lost focus away from the screen. A few tips that I used was to set a cooking timer for 20 minutes and when it goes off, get my head out of the screen for a few minutes. That helped. What also helped is to not read while scrolling. That was a bad habit that I picked up somewhere along the line. Fonts that appear in focus on a screen when stationary, can be quite skewed and out of focus when moving during a scroll. And trying to read them, your brain subliminally tries to correct them. And of course eye drops in the morning. 
My eyes are almost back to normal - this light might help to keep them that way considering I still do about 10 hours a day on screen.

I'm sorry to curb your enthusiasm: the magnetic connection design is flawed, a fact you'll only notice with time. I bought this product back in October 2020 and, after a year or so of use, the lamp started to frequently flick due to bad contact of the metal plates. I was usually able to fix it by moving the bar a bit, but the problem only got worse and now, after two years, it's almost impossible to use the lamp. I suspect this problem was caused by the metal cylindrical body of the lamp having its shape microscopically distorted due to weather variations so that now it won't fit perfectly with the monitor support, affecting the magnetic connection and the proper touching of metal contacts. I'm a big fan of Xiaomi in general, but in this case, the experience was very disappointing!
